=== SPSC Ring Buffer Memory Ordering Test ===
--- Building with ThreadSanitizer ---
rm -f ringbuf_test ringbuf_test_tsan
gcc -std=c11 -pthread -Wall -Wextra -Wpedantic -fsanitize=thread -O1 -g -o ringbuf_test_tsan ring_buffer.c main.c

--- Running ThreadSanitizer stress test ---

NOTE: ThreadSanitizer cannot run in this container (ASLR/personality restriction).
      Falling back to multi-run functional correctness test.

/*
 * main.c — SPSC ring buffer stress test.
 *
 * Producer thread pushes N sequential integers (0..N-1) into the ring
 * buffer; consumer thread pops them and verifies strict sequential
 * ordering plus data integrity. On a correct implementation, all N
 * items arrive in order with no gaps and no corruption.
 *
 * Compile with:
 *   gcc -std=c11 -pthread -O2 -o ringbuf_test ring_buffer.c main.c
 *
 * For ThreadSanitizer:
 *   gcc -std=c11 -pthread -fsanitize=thread -O1 -g -o ringbuf_test ring_

/*
 * ring_buffer.h — Lock-free single-producer single-consumer (SPSC) ring buffer.
 *
 * Design notes:
 *   - Power-of-two capacity for fast modular indexing (bitwise AND).
 *   - Atomic indices enable wait-free push/pop without mutexes.
 *   - resize() protected by a full memory fence to ensure internal
 *     consistency of the capacity/mask fields during reallocation.
 *   - Designed for embedded/low-latency pipelines where throughput
 *     matters more than generality (single producer, sin

=== SPSC Ring Buffer Test ===
